content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "collaboration",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 52,
    "domain_depth": 35,
    "data_richness": 28,
    "interaction_quality": 45,
    "ai_integration": 5,
    "mobile_first": 38,
    "performance": 42,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 50,
    "realtime": 55,
    "accessibility": 40,
    "security": 48,
    "differentiation": 35,
    "enterprise_readiness": 30
  },
  "overall_score": 40,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C4-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "enterprise_readiness",
      "title": "No team directory page or crew management UI exists",
      "file": "src/pages/Directory.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires a full Directory page with contact cards, company grouping, search, import/export, linked data, and insurance status. No such page exists. This is a P0 gap for any GC managing 30+ subs on a project. The Collaboration module must provide the team roster and contact infrastructure.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Directory.tsx, create a new page component called DirectoryPage. BEFORE: The file either does not exist or contains a placeholder with no real functionality — no searchable contact list, no company grouping, no contact cards with click-to-call/email, no linked RFI/task data. AFTER: Build a production Directory page with these sections: (1) A search bar at top that filters contacts by name, company, email, phone, role, and trade with debounced 200ms input. (2) Metric cards row showing Total Contacts, Active Companies, Expiring Certifications, Missing Insurance counts. (3) A toggle between List View (table with columns: Name, Company, Role, Trade, Phone, Email, Status) and Company View (grouped cards). (4) Each contact row is clickable and shows a slide-out detail panel with full contact info, linked RFIs where they are ball-in-court, assigned tasks, and meeting attendance. (5) Phone numbers render as click-to-call links (tel:), emails as mailto: links. (6) Empty state: clipboard illustration icon, message 'Build your project directory. Add every stakeholder so your team always knows who to call.' with CTA buttons 'Add First Contact' and 'Import from CSV'. (7) Use useProjectMembers or useCrews hooks to fetch from project_members joined with users table via supabase. (8) All styling uses inline styles from src/styles/theme.ts tokens — no CSS modules. (9) Add aria-label on search input, role='table' on the list, and aria-live='polite' on the results count. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C4-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No meetings page with agenda, minutes, and action item tracking",
      "file": "src/pages/Meetings.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA specifies a full Meetings page with series support, agenda management, collaborative note-taking, action item carry-forward, attendance tracking, and minutes PDF generation. No meetings page exists. OAC meetings are a weekly contractual requirement on every commercial project.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Meetings.tsx, create a new page component called MeetingsPage. BEFORE: The file either does not exist or shows only a placeholder with no meeting management functionality. AFTER: Build a production Meetings page with: (1) Metric cards: Upcoming Meetings count, Open Action Items count, Meetings This Week, Average Attendance %. (2) A list/table of meetings with columns: Date, Title, Type (OAC/internal/subcontractor/safety as colored Tag pills), Attendees count, Action Items (open/total), Status (scheduled/completed). Each row clickable to detail. (3) 'Schedule Meeting' button opening a modal form with fields: Title (required), Description, Date/Time picker, Location, Duration (minutes), Type dropdown, Attendees multi-select from project directory. Form validates on blur. (4) Meeting detail panel (slide from right, 560px) showing: agenda items as ordered list, discussion notes per item, action items with assignee/due date/status, attendance checklist. (5) Action items section: each item shows description, assignee avatar+name, due date with aging color (green >4 days, amber 1-4, red overdue), status toggle. (6) Empty state: calendar icon, 'No meetings scheduled. Set up your recurring OAC meeting or schedule a one off.' with 'Schedule Meeting' CTA. (7) Query meetings table via supabase with real-time subscription. (8) Use theme tokens for all styles. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C4-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "PresenceBar relies on Liveblocks useOthers but has no graceful degradation when Liveblocks is not configured",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx",
      "description": "PresenceBar imports useOthers from liveblocks. If Liveblocks room is not configured or throws, the error boundary shows a generic 'Presence unavailable' message with a reload button. This is not graceful — it should silently render nothing rather than showing a broken state, and should populate the presence store with mock/empty data so downstream PresenceDots components do not break.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, in the PresenceBarErrorBoundary class and the inner PresenceBarContent component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The error boundary renders a visible 'Presence unavailable' message with a reload button when Liveblocks fails, which looks broken to end users. The inner component calls useOthers() without a try-catch pattern and if the Liveblocks provider is missing it throws. AFTER: (1) In PresenceBarErrorBoundary.render(), when hasError is true, return null instead of the error message div — presence is a nice-to-have, not essential, so failing silently is correct. (2) Add a check at the top of the inner PresenceBarContent component: wrap the useOthers call in a try-catch by creating a safe wrapper. Before the component that uses useOthers, add a parent component SafePresenceBar that checks if the Liveblocks room context exists using a try-catch around useOthers — if it throws, set a ref flag and render null. (3) Log the error once with console.debug (not console.warn) to reduce noise. (4) Ensure presenceStore.setInitialized(true) is still called even on failure so downstream PresenceDots components render their empty state correctly rather than showing loading indicators forever. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C4-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "EditConflictGuard checkConflict has a string interpolation syntax error and inconsistent retry logic",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx",
      "description": "The checkConflict function in useOptimisticLock has a broken template literal (backslash-n visible in source) and the retry logic only retries once but the error toast says 'Refresh the page' which is overly aggressive. The isChecking state is never set back to false in the error path, causing a perpetual loading state.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, in the useOptimisticLock hook's checkConflict callback, make these specific changes: BEFORE: (1) The code contains a malformed template literal with backslash-n characters in the source that would cause syntax issues. (2) isChecking is set to true at the start but never set to false in the catch/error branches — only the happy path presumably sets it false. (3) The error toast tells users to 'Refresh the page and try again' which is disruptive for a conflict check failure. AFTER: (1) Fix any malformed string literals — ensure all template literals use proper backtick syntax. (2) Add a finally block after the try-catch that always calls setIsChecking(false) to prevent perpetual loading state. (3) Change the error toast message from 'Could not verify edits. Refresh the page and try again.' to 'Could not verify if others have edited this item. Your changes may overwrite recent updates.' with type 'warning' instead of 'error'. (4) Change retryCountRef max from 1 to 2 (try up to 3 total attempts) with the delay increasing: 500ms first retry, 1500ms second retry. (5) In the conflict detected path (when server updated_at differs from lastKnownUpdatedAt), ensure setIsChecking(false) is called before returning true. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C4-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "EntityPresence overflow dropdown not keyboard accessible and missing ARIA roles",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx",
      "description": "The PresenceDots component has an overflow dropdown that opens on click but the dropdown list items lack role='listbox' and role='option'. The individual user dot buttons have min 44px touch targets (good) but the overflow count button that shows '+N' does not announce the expanded state. The overflow dropdown also has no focus trap or Escape-to-close handler.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, in the PresenceDots component, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The overflow button (showing '+N more') toggles overflowOpen state but lacks aria-expanded, aria-haspopup attributes. The dropdown that appears has no role='listbox' on the container or role='option' on items. There is no Escape key handler to close the dropdown. The handleOverflowBlur handles focus-out but Escape is not handled. AFTER: (1) On the overflow count button, add aria-expanded={overflowOpen}, aria-haspopup='listbox', and update the aria-label to include the count: `${overflow} more viewers`. (2) On the dropdown container div, add role='listbox' and aria-label='Additional viewers'. (3) On each item in the overflow dropdown, add role='option'. (4) Add an onKeyDown handler to the overflow container div that calls setOverflowOpen(false) when e.key === 'Escape' and e.stopPropagation(). (5) When overflowOpen becomes true, focus the first item in the dropdown using a useEffect with a ref. (6) Ensure the outer container div has role='group' with aria-label='Active viewers on this item'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Meeting Minutes AI Auto-Generation",
      "description": "After a meeting ends, one-click generates formatted minutes from the agenda items, notes, and action items using the AI copilot edge function. Outputs a PDF matching construction industry standard OAC meeting minute format with attendee list, old/new business, and action item table. Saves PM 30+ minutes per weekly OAC meeting.",
      "effort": "medium",
      "impact": "high"
    },
    {
      "title": "Subcontractor Scorecard Dashboard",
      "description": "Aggregates each subcontractor's performance across RFI response time, punch item closure rate, submittal resubmission frequency, safety incident count, and schedule adherence. Shows a letter grade (A through F) per sub per project, with portfolio-wide trending. This data informs future bidding decisions — a killer differentiator no competitor offers at this depth.",
      "effort": "high",
      "impact": "high"
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's Directory module is functional but static — no linked RFI/task data on contact cards, no sub scorecards, no real-time presence showing who is viewing what. Fieldwire has no directory or meeting management at all. Buildertrend has basic contact lists but no company-level insurance tracking. SiteSync's real-time presence layer is ahead of all competitors but the lack of a Directory page and Meetings page means the collaboration module is incomplete for enterprise GC workflows. The edit conflict guard pattern is a genuine differentiator — no competitor shows real-time edit locks.",
  "summary": "The Collaboration module has solid technical foundations — real-time presence via Liveblocks, optimistic edit conflict detection, and entity-level presence dots are all genuinely differentiated capabilities. However, two critical pages are missing entirely: Directory (the project phone book that every GC relies on daily) and Meetings (OAC meeting management with minutes and action items, a contractual requirement on commercial projects). These are P0 gaps per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA. The existing components have quality issues: PresenceBar fails loudly instead of gracefully when Liveblocks is unavailable, EditConflictGuard has a state management bug that causes perpetual loading, and EntityPresence lacks proper ARIA attributes for keyboard accessibility. The module scores 40 overall — the architecture is promising but the feature surface area covers only 40% of what a GC needs from a collaboration system. Building the Directory and Meetings pages would immediately jump this to 60+."
}
```
